mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which compresses input costs and lifts short-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations during tightening cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or 60-day returns show no significant correlation with payroll expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms robust economic momentum, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Combined GDP and consumption growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly contractions in industrial production signal supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below previous month value for 2 consecutive periods
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 40% of consecutive INDPRO decline periods, or the signal produces positive returns during late-cycle phases.
